tasks.title: Add real-time ball speed HUD indicator
tasks.description: Add a speed indicator HUD element in the lower-left corner that displays the ball's current speed (magnitude of vx/vz velocity) during active gameplay. The indicator should be hidden on start/game-over screens and match the existing HUD styling.
tasks.acceptance_criteria:
1. A #speed div is visible in the lower-left corner during gameplay.
2. Speed updates every frame using Math.sqrt(vx² + vz²), rounded to 1 decimal.
3. Display format includes a unit label (e.g. '2.4 m/s').
4. Hidden on start screen, game-over screen; visible only during active play.
5. Visual style matches the existing #score HUD element (font, color, background pill, text-shadow).

checklist.description: Add #speed div element to index.html body
steps:
Add
0.0 m/s
after the #score div in index.html body
Verify the element is present in the HTML

checklist.description: Add CSS styling for #speed that matches existing HUD style and positions it in the lower-left
steps: Add #speed CSS rule in the <style> block with position:fixed, bottom:16px, left:16px
Match font-family, color, text-shadow, background, padding, border-radius from #score
Set font-size to 1.2em, font-weight 700, z-index 10, pointer-events none
Set display:none as default (hidden until gameplay starts)

checklist.description: Add speedEl DOM reference and show/hide logic in main.js
steps: Add const speedEl = document.getElementById('speed') with other DOM refs
In init(), add speedEl.style.display = 'block' alongside scoreEl display
In enterGameOver(), add speedEl.style.display = 'none'
In exitGameOver(), add speedEl.style.display = 'block'

checklist.description: Compute and display ball speed each frame in gameLoop
steps: After updatePhysics() result, compute speed = Math.sqrt(result.vx * result.vx + result.vz * result.vz)
Update speedEl.textContent = speed.toFixed(1) + ' m/s'
Verify speed updates continuously during playing and falling states

checklist.description: Speed indicator is hidden on start and game-over screens
steps: Verify #speed starts with display:none in CSS (hidden on start screen)
Verify enterGameOver() hides #speed
Verify exitGameOver() re-shows #speed

quality_checklist.description: Speed indicator CSS is consistent with existing HUD elements (#score)
steps: Compare #speed CSS properties against #score CSS properties
Verify same font-family, color, text-shadow, background, padding, border-radius values
Verify position/size differences are intentional (bottom-left vs top-left, slightly smaller font)

quality_checklist.description: No unnecessary code additions — minimal diff, no over-engineering
steps: Check that only index.html and js/main.js were modified
Verify no new functions or abstractions were created beyond what's needed
Verify no changes to physics.js, renderer.js, or tracker.js

quality_checklist.description: Speed computation is correct and uses proper formula
steps: Verify Math.sqrt(vx*vx + vz*vz) is used (not just vx+vz or similar)
Verify .toFixed(1) is used for 1 decimal place
Verify unit label 'm/s' is included in the display text

checks.description: Speed indicator is visible in the lower-left corner during gameplay
steps: Open the game at http://localhost:8080
Grant camera access and wait for gameplay to start
Look for a speed readout (e.g. '2.0 m/s') in the lower-left corner
Verify it has a semi-transparent dark background pill matching the score style

checks.description: Speed value updates in real-time and responds to game mechanics
steps: During gameplay, observe the speed value changing as the ball moves
Verify the value is formatted with 1 decimal place and 'm/s' unit
If turtle powerup is collected, verify speed value decreases (slowdown effect)

checks.description: Speed indicator is hidden on start and game-over screens
steps: On initial page load (before camera access), verify no speed indicator is visible
During gameplay, collide with an obstacle to trigger game over
Verify the speed indicator disappears on the game-over screen
After game over dismisses and gameplay resumes, verify speed indicator reappears
